visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
Primary topics: films "Baby" (2007) / "October Baby" and actor birthdates (Oct 20, 1971)
- Facts: Feodor Chin (born August 18, 1974) "starred as the antagonist 'Benny' in Juwan Chung's 'Baby'"; Chris Sligh (born July 20, 1978) "starred in 'October Baby'"; Matthew Godfrey (born October 20, 1971) is a Canadian actor.
- Missing bridge fact: whether an American actor born Oct 20, 1971 starred in either "Baby" (2007) or "October Baby". Possible bridge entities: Matthew Godfrey (born Oct 20, 1971) — passage lists him as Canadian and does not show credits for these films (nationality/film-credit unverified); Chris Sligh — passage confirms he starred in "October Baby" but his birthdate (July 20, 1978) does not match Oct 20, 1971.
- Next-hop retrievals (priority order): 1) "October Baby" Wikipedia page — lede/infobox and "Cast" section; 2) "Baby (2007 film)" or "Baby (Juwan Chung)" Wikipedia page — lede/infobox and "Cast" section; 3) "Matthew Godfrey" page — lede/infobox and filmography/credits; 4) site:wikipedia.org search for "born October 20, 1971" + "actor" + "American".

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Priority rules for ambiguity and disambiguation:
- If summary_1 lists multiple candidate anchors or otherwise signals ambiguity, emit a single connector-style query that concatenates the prioritized anchor tokens (use the order or priority visible in summary_1) and then append exactly one short attribute token or a visible year token for disambiguation.
- If summary_1 contains a comma-separated list of individuals and the missing evidence concerns a member, prefer the earliest-listed individual token paired with the visible group token and append the single attribute token member.
- If the question contains a visible year token that disambiguates a title, include that year token before the attribute token in the query.
- If none of the above applies, emit a single-anchor query using the most relevant visible token and at most one short attribute token when the question implies an attribute.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.

retrieved_titles: Chara the Best Baby Baby Baby XXX
retrieved_doc_snippets: Casa de los Babys | Casa de los Babys ("House of the Babies") is a 2003 drama film written, directed, and edited by filmmaker John Sayles. It features an ensemble cast, including Marcia Gay Harden, Maggie Gyllenhaal and Daryl Hannah.

War Babies (film) | War Babies is a 1932 American comedy short film directed by Charles Lamont and is the second in a series of eight one-reelers that satirized adult films and themes called "Baby Burlesks". The casts in the series are pre-schoolers dressed in adult costumes on top and diapers fastened with large safety pins on the bottom. In her autobiography, Shirley Temple Black describes the "Baby Burlesks" series as "a cynical exploitation of our childish innocence," and notes the short fil ...[truncated]

Mighty Baby (film) | Mighty Baby (絕世好B) is a 2002 Hong Kong comedy film directed by Chan Hing-Ka and Patrick Leung. A sequel to the 2001 film, "La Brassiere", the film stars returning cast members Lau Ching-wan, Louis Koo, Carina Lau and Gigi Leung alongside new cast members Cecilia Cheung and Rosamund Kwan

Toronto hospital baby deaths | The Toronto hospital baby deaths were multiple alleged poisonings of babies at Toronto's Hospital for Sick Children that occurred between June 1980 and April 1981, when charges of murder were laid against a nurse at the hospital. The story was a major news event throughout the year, and ended with the nurse being exonerated. Although suspicion was cast on other people, no further charges were ever laid.

Chara the Best Baby Baby Baby XXX | Chara the Best Baby Baby Baby XXX (チャラ・ザ・ベスト・ベイビー・ベイビー・ベイビー , Chara za Besuto Beibī Beibī Beibī ) is the first best album of Chara, which was released on October 10, 1995. It features material from her first four studio albums, Sweet, Soul Kiss, Violet Blue and Happy Toy; as well as some new material. It debuted at #5 on the Japanese Oricon album charts, and charted in the top 200 for 13 weeks. It eventually sold 190,000 copies.

Baby K | Stephanie Keene (October 13, 1992 – April 5, 1995), better known by the pseudonym Baby K, was an anencephalic baby who became the center of a major U.S. court case and a debate among bioethicists.

Dirty Baby | Dirty Baby (stylized as "DIRTY BABY") is an album by American guitarist Nels Cline performing compositions inspired by Edward Ruscha which was released in October 2010 on the Cryptogramophone label.

output.query: Baby (2007 film) Kenneth Choi Baby stars which American actor born on October 20, 1971?

retrieved_doc_snippets: Baby (2007 film) | Baby is a 2007 independent film, considered part of the hood film genre. The film tells the story of an Asian-American youth's gang life in East Los Angeles, set during the mid '80s to the early '90s. Directed by Juwan Chung and starring David Huynh, Tzi Ma, Feodor Chin, Ron Yuan and Kenneth Choi. It has been called "the Asian American Boyz n the Hood" by the San Francisco Chronicle. The film won Best Narrative Feature at the 2007 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival and a  ...[truncated]

Kenneth Choi | Kenneth Choi (born October 20, 1971) is an American actor. He is best known for playing the character Henry Lin on the television series "Sons of Anarchy" (2008–2014). He is also known for his roles as Jim Morita in the film "" (2011), Chester Ming in Martin Scorsese's "The Wolf of Wall Street" (2013), Judge Lance Ito in "" (2016). and Lewis on the FOX comedy series "The Last Man on Earth".

Feodor Chin | Feodor Chin (born August 18, 1974) is an American actor, writer, voiceover artist, producer and director. Chin starred as the antagonist "Benny" in Juwan Chung's "Baby" opposite David Huynh, the protagonist. "Baby" won a Special Jury Award for Best Feature Length Film, Narrative at the 2007 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival, among other awards. He also starred as pioneering actor and artist Keye Luke in Timothy Tau's short film bio-pic "Keye Luke", which premiered at the 2012 ...[truncated]

Coffin Baby | Coffin Baby (aka Coffin Baby - The Toolbox Killer Is Back) is a 2013 American horror film written and directed by Dean C. Jones and starring Bruce Dern, Brian Krause, Clifton Powell, and Ethan Phillips. It has been represented as a sequel to 2004's "Toolbox Murders", but is not. The majority of the footage in the film was shot for "Toolbox Murders 2", the sequel to the 2004 film in which the eponymous toolbox killer, Coffin Baby, resumes his gruesome killing spree from the first fi ...[truncated]

Juwan Chung | Juwan Chung (born 1983) is an American director and writer. His gangland film "Baby" won a Special Jury Prize for Best Feature, Narrative at the 2007 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival and lead Actor David Huynh won a Special Jury Prize at the 2007 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival for Outstanding Newcomer and Best Emerging Actor for his performance in the film. For "Baby", Chung also won Best Director at the 2008 DisOrient Film Festival.

Michael Starke (actor) | Michael Starke (born 13 November 1957) is a British actor best known for his role as Thomas 'Sinbad' Sweeney which he played for 16 years in the soap opera "Brookside". Starke then appeared in the ITV drama "The Royal" as Kenneth Hopkirk, as policeman Arthur in the film "The 51st State" and as himself on Lily Savage's Blankety Blank. He is a patron of Zöe's Place Baby Hospice, a charity for sick babies and young children.

My Baby Is Missing | My Baby Is Missing is a made-for-television film which aired on Lifetime in 2007, and stars Gina Philips as a career-driven woman determined to prove that her newborn baby wasn't stillborn, but stolen and sold.
target_state.summary_2: Kenneth Choi is the American actor born October 20, 1971 who starred in Baby. Kenneth Choi is best known for playing Henry Lin on the television series Sons of Anarchy.
